context.projectOverview: agent-bober is a multi-agent sprint harness (TypeScript, ESM, strict TS). All LLM calls go through provider adapters behind providers/types.ts. This sprint adds Anthropic prompt caching (cache_control breakpoints) to the AnthropicAdapter, gated by a providerConfig.promptCaching flag that defaults ON for Anthropic and is a no-op for other providers. It is borrow #1 from a study of nousresearch/hermes-agent (agent/prompt_caching.py 'system_and_3').
relevantFiles: src/providers/anthropic.ts — the adapter to modify (constructor ~line 133, chat() ~line 140)
src/providers/factory.ts — anthropic case (~lines 159-161) resolves promptCaching from providerConfig and passes it to the adapter
src/providers/types.ts — LLMClient / ChatParams / Message shapes (do NOT add cache fields to ChatResponse.usage this sprint)
src/providers/openai.ts — reference for how an adapter already receives providerConfig from the factory
src/providers/openai.test.ts and src/providers/google.test.ts — reference vitest patterns for a new src/providers/anthropic.test.ts
src/config/schema.ts — per-role providerConfig is z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()); promptCaching is a free-form key, NO migration needed

keyPrinciples: ESM everywhere: all relative imports use .js extensions (NodeNext). The new test file imports './anthropic.js', './factory.js', etc.
No SDK lock-in: @anthropic-ai/sdk may ONLY be imported inside src/providers/anthropic.ts (and its test). Never leak Anthropic SDK types outside the adapter.
Prefix unused params with `_`; aim for zero `any` (use unknown + narrowing).
Vitest tests collocated as *.test.ts next to source. Mocking the @anthropic-ai/sdk module (vi.mock) is appropriate here — the 'no filesystem mocks' principle is about fs, not the LLM SDK.
